mnemonics: FANBOYS for AND: Think 'False And Not Both Or Yes?' to remember AND is only true when both are true.
NOR is NOT OR: Remember that NOR is the negation (NOT) of the OR operation.
The 'p' in 'implies' is a Promise: If the promise (p) is true, it must be kept (q must be true) to be a true statement.
keyPoints: The AND (∧) connective is only true when both propositions are true.
The OR (∨) connective is inclusive; it is true when at least one proposition is true.
The implication (→) 'p implies q' is false only when p is true and q is false.
commonMistakes: Confusing the inclusive OR (∨) with the exclusive OR (⊕); OR allows both to be true, XOR does not.
Misinterpreting the implication (→) as causality; it is defined only by the truth values of p and q, not a real-world cause-and-effect.
Forgetting that a conditional statement is vacuously true when the hypothesis (p) is false, regardless of the truth value of q.
